The simulation begins at t=0 seconds and runs until t = 1000 seconds with a time step of 1 second, and results are written at intervals of every 50 seconds. One wall has a temperature of 305 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K.
Refer to the following similar case file content if helpful:

<file_name>fvSolution</file_name>
<folder_name>system</folder_name>
<content>{solvers
{
    U
    {
        solver          PBiCG;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-07;
        relTol          0.05;
    }
}
    PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors 1;
    nCorrectors 2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    maxIter 100;
    minIter 3;
    mergeTolerance 1e-07;
}
    SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    nCorrectors 1;
    nRelaxIterations 2;
    relaxationFactors
    {
        fields
        {
            U
            {
                field   U
                relaxationFactor 0.3;
            }
            p
            {
                field   p
                relaxationFactor 0.3;
            }
        }
        equations
        {
            U
            {
                field   U
                relaxationFactor 0.7;
            }
            p
            {
                field   p
                relaxationFactor 0.3;
            }
        }
    }
}
    transient
{
    scheme          Euler;
}</content>

<file_name>fvSolution</file_name>
<folder_name>system</folder_name>
<content>{solvers
{
    U
    {
        solver          PBiCG;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-07;
        relTol          0.05;
    }
}
    PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors 1;
    nCorrectors 2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    maxIter 100;
    minIter 3;
    mergeTolerance 1e-07;
}
    SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    nCorrectors 1;
    nRelaxIterations 2;
    relaxationFactors
    {
        fields
        {
            U
            {
                field   U
                relaxationFactor 0.3;
            }
            p
            {
                field   p
                relaxationFactor 0.3;
            }
        }
        equations
        {
            U
            {
                field   U
                relaxationFactor 0.7;
            }
            p
            {
                field   p
                relaxationFactor 0.3;
            }
        }
    }
}
    transient
{
    scheme          Euler;
}</content>

Usage: blockMesh [OPTIONS]
options:
  -blockTopology    write block edges and centres as .obj files

                    do not execute functionObjects
  -region <name>    specify alternative mesh region
  -srcDoc           display source code in browser
  -doc              display application documentation in browser
  -help             print the usage

Block description

  For a given block, the correspondence between the ordering of
  vertex labels and face labels is shown below.
  For vertex numbering in the sequence 0 to 7 (block, centre):
    faces 0 (f0) and 1 are left and right, respectively;
    faces 2 and 3 are front and back; 
    and faces 4 and 5 are bottom and top::

                 7 ---- 6
            f5   |\     |\   f3
            |    | 4 ---- 5   \
            |    3 |--- 2 |    \
            |     \|     \|    f2
            f4     0 ---- 1

       Z         f0 ----- f1
       |  Y
       | /
       O --- X
